advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Sustainable weight management requires a behavioral, metabolic, and nutritional multi-pronged approach. Caloric restriction must be combined with protein preservation strategies and physical activity to prevent lean mass loss.
research_findings: Evidence confirms that a 500-750 kcal daily deficit produces safe, sustainable weight loss of 0.5-0.75 kg/week. High-protein dietary patterns (1.2-1.6g/kg) during caloric restriction preserve lean muscle mass.
current_focus_areas: Building weight management protocols segmented by BMI class, age, and comorbidity profile; researching plateau-breaking dietary strategies; integrating metabolic adaptation management.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Performance & Metabolic Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 9 (Sports Nutrition). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 62%.
Progress update: Completed weight management protocols for 5 BMI classification tiers. Validated lean mass preservation strategies under caloric restriction conditions. Progress advanced to 67%.
Phase transition: advanced from 'independent_learning' to 'collaborative_learning'. Bot 10 now entering collaborative learning phase. Progress: 75%.
